qa.user_verbatim_complaint: 现在如果遇到没有实现的ticket，因为过于简单我使用/do来开发的，出现了无法close的现象。先调查为什么

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Step 1 of the forced-override path captures --reason text but does not explicitly state that the --reason token sequence is stripped from the remaining argument before task-id resolution. This could create ambiguity for a literal implementer. Pre-existing wording issue, not introduced by this cycle (classified out_of_scope by dev-report codex_consult round 1 finding #1).

The 'When to use' section for --force does not include the fourth bullet 'Direct /do development that did not go through /dev' (R3 Should Have in BA spec). This is a Should Have requirement that was not encoded in any acceptance criterion. Not blocking.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: The root cause was a two-part gap: (1) close.md line 62 'reused identically; do NOT branch' language caused forced-path to inherit qa-report verification, and (2) do.md had no post-development workflow guidance. Both gaps are now closed: line 62 replacement text separates form-detection from artifact verification; line 139 sub-branches explicitly gate qa-report check on non-force path only; do.md has '## Closing /do-developed work' section with escape hatch command and /commit follow-up.

complaint_element_pointer.selector: N/A — doc-only change, no DOM element
complaint_element_pointer.how_located: N/A — slash command files verified via filesystem read

qa.overall_assessment: Both acceptance criteria fully satisfied. AC1: close.md line 62 old wording 'reused identically; do NOT branch the resolution logic' is confirmed removed and replaced with text separating form-detection from artifact verification, plus explicit forced-path skip clause for qa-report. Line 139 timestamp branch is restructured into explicit non-force/forced sub-branches eliminating the 'Verify both exist' ambiguity. No textual contradiction between line 62 and line 139. Normal-path unchanged (line 155 preflight still requires all 5 artifacts). AC2: do.md has new '## Closing /do-developed work' section covering all three required elements. AC3: git diff HEAD shows only commands/close.md and commands/do.md contain this cycle's targeted edits; other staged changes are pre-existing from prior cycles.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: 出现了无法close的现象 (there is a phenomenon where they can't be closed)
success_criteria_results.criterion: /close <ts> --force succeeds when only ticket-<ts>.md exists (no qa-report)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read commands/close.md lines 59-62 and 137-142; verified via grep; confirmed via git diff HEAD
success_criteria_results.details: Line 62 now reads: 'Reuse the same task-id form-detection and TASK_ID derivation rules below; do NOT create a separate path/timestamp/no-argument resolution algorithm for --force. Artifact existence checks are path-specific: when this resolution is invoked from the Forced-override path, skip qa-report-<task-id>.json verification.' Line 139 sub-branches: non-force requires qa-report; forced-override skips it. Old 'Verify both exist' wording is gone.
success_criteria_results.evidence: grep 'Forced-override path, skip' commands/close.md returns line 62 and line 141. grep 'Verify both exist' returns no match. grep 'Non-force path' returns line 140. grep 'Forced-override path.*do NOT resolve' returns line 141.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: 先调查为什么 (investigate why first)
success_criteria_results.criterion: commands/do.md documents the /close procedure for /do-developed tickets
success_criteria_results.details: do.md now has '## Closing /do-developed work' section (lines 8-26) containing: (1) explanation that /do creates no dev cycle artifacts and /close normal path will fail; (2) escape hatch command: /close docs/dev/ticket-<task-id>.md --force --reason 'developed with /do'; (3) note that path form is always safe, timestamp form also works in forced mode; (4) edge case: if no ticket file exists, create one first; (5) follow-up: /commit <task-id> -m '<summary>'
success_criteria_results.evidence: grep 'Closing /do-developed work' commands/do.md: line 8. grep 'Escape hatch' commands/do.md: line 12. grep '/commit.*task-id.*summary' commands/do.md: line 25.

qa._success_criteria_rule: Both R1 and R2 from requirements_decomposition verified.

root_cause_verification.rationale: Root cause A (close.md forced path inherited qa-report check): The 'reused identically' language at line 62 is confirmed removed and replaced with text explicitly scoping qa-report skip to forced path. The timestamp branch at line 139 restructures the check into non-force/forced sub-branches. Root cause B (do.md had no workflow documentation): Confirmed fixed by new section.

build_verification.skip_reason: doc-only change — no executable code, no deployed service, no frontend, no backend pipeline. Both modified files are slash-command documentation files (.md) read by the orchestrator at invocation time. No build or deploy step exists or is relevant.

regression_test_results.details: close.md line 155 preflight section still requires all 5 artifacts: ticket, context, dev-report, qa-report (nested qa.status==pass), and completion. The forced-path short-circuit at line 157 explicitly states '/close --force short-circuits before this normal-path preflight.' The normal path is unchanged.

close.md line 138 path-form resolution (explicit .md/.json argument) derives task-id from basename only. No qa-report check exists for path form. This form was already safe before this cycle and remains safe.

regression_test_results.test: Forced path still writes close-report even without upstream artifacts
regression_test_results.details: close.md line 94 still reads 'This file MUST be written even if the task-id has no upstream BA spec / context / dev-report (per AC-CLOSE-FORCE-6).' This behavior is unchanged.
do.md line 3 still has 'disable-model-invocation: true'. The new '## Closing /do-developed work' section is appended after the hook-only body line, preserving the frontmatter and existing content.

e2e_enforcement.blocking_reason: doc-only change — no user-triggered code path, no UI, no backend service, no deployed artifact modified. Both modified files are slash-command documentation files (.md) with disable-model-invocation:true; context.setup.applicability is N/A per BA spec. Playwright verification does not apply per agents/qa.md skip condition: change affects nothing the user can see or trigger in a browser.

ui_test_results.reason: legitimately_skipped: doc-only change — context.setup.applicability is N/A per BA spec; no rendered UI changes, no browser interaction, no Playwright invocation, no user-triggered code paths affected. Only slash-command documentation files modified.

ac1_verification_detail.line_62_old_text: The task-id resolution rules are reused identically; do NOT branch the resolution logic.
ac1_verification_detail.line_62_new_text: Reuse the same task-id form-detection and TASK_ID derivation rules below; do NOT create a separate path/timestamp/no-argument resolution algorithm for --force. Artifact existence checks are path-specific: when this resolution is invoked from the Forced-override path, skip qa-report-<task-id>.json verification.
ac1_verification_detail.line_139_old_text: Also resolve docs/dev/qa-report-${ARGUMENTS}.json. Verify both exist.
ac1_verification_detail.line_139_new_structure: Sub-branches: ticket/ba-spec check always applied; non-force path adds qa-report check; forced-override path skips qa-report
ac1_verification_detail.contradiction_check: Line 62 says do NOT create a separate form-detection algorithm (shared Elif branch in task-id resolution confirms form-detection is still shared). Line 62 also says artifact checks are path-specific on forced path. Line 139 uses the SAME Elif timestamp branch for both paths but gates qa-report check on non-force sub-branch. No contradiction — form-detection is shared, artifact verification is path-conditioned within the shared branch.
ac1_verification_detail.normal_path_evidence: Line 155: preflight still requires all 5 artifacts. Line 157: forced path short-circuits before this preflight.

ac2_verification_detail.element_1_evidence: Line 10: '/do only writes /tmp/claude-orchestrator-consent-<session-id>.flag; it does not create any dev cycle artifacts (no context, dev-report, qa-report, or completion). The /close normal path requires the full /dev artifact chain and will fail without it.'
ac2_verification_detail.element_3_evidence: Line 18: 'The explicit path form (docs/dev/ticket-<task-id>.md) is always safe — path-form resolution only verifies the named file exists, not qa-report. Bare timestamp form (/close <task-id> --force) also works in forced mode when the ticket or legacy spec file exists; forced mode does not require qa-report.'
ac2_verification_detail.element_4_evidence: Lines 23-25: 'After a successful forced close, follow up with: /commit <task-id> -m "<summary>"'

ac3_verification_detail.other_staged_files_source: pre-existing staged changes from prior cycles already in git index before this cycle began
ac3_verification_detail.evidence: git diff HEAD -- commands/close.md commands/do.md confirms both files contain exactly the targeted edits described in the dev report. git diff HEAD for this cycle touches only these two files. The large set of staged changes visible in git status are from prior auto-bulk commits and were in the index before this cycle's dev work.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 3 findings. Issue 1 (--reason stripping ambiguity): pre-existing wording in step 1, not introduced by this cycle; classified out_of_scope (also pre-classified as out_of_scope in dev-report codex_consult round 1). Issue 2 (AC3 verifiability from current tree): git diff HEAD against target files confirms only two files changed by this cycle; other staged/untracked items are pre-existing work; AC3 satisfied. Issue 3 (R3 Should Have not implemented): R3 is a Should Have in BA spec, not encoded in any acceptance criterion; cannot block the pass verdict; classified out_of_scope.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Issue 1: out_of_scope (pre-existing). Issue 2: AC3 evidence strengthened with explicit git diff HEAD citation showing only the two target files. Issue 3: out_of_scope (Should Have, not in any AC).
